brand.name: Statements by J
brand.vertical: Home furnishings e-commerce (online nationwide + appointment showroom in Commerce, CA)

summary: Statements by J is a Los Angeles-based home-furnishings retailer selling modern, designer-aesthetic furniture (dining, living, bedroom, office, entryway, and small-space pieces) online with nationwide shipping from its Commerce, CA warehouse, plus an appointment-only showroom. It serves style-conscious homeowners aged 25-55 in major US metros who want a high-end look without luxury markups, with a secondary audience of interior designers sourcing for client projects. Revenue is one-off repeat purchases with no subscription or trial; growth depends on converting its email list and pulling past buyers back to furnish additional rooms.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Convert new email signups into first-time buyers by delivering the 15% code, establishing the brand story, and defusing the #1 objection (can't touch it before buying).
campaigns.trigger: Email signup / first-order discount opt-in (send email 1 within seconds).
campaigns.segment: New contacts who joined the list; branch later by category browsed (living, dining, bedroom, small-space).
campaigns.rationale: The 15% code is the brand's main capture mechanism but is likely delivered as a bare transactional email; this is the highest-engagement, fastest-ROI flow and the only place to turn a discount-hunter into a confident buyer despite the no-touch online risk.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome: deliver the 15% code, land the anti-markup brand story, and remove the no-touch buying risk so the first order happens.
emails.subject: Welcome in — here's 15% off (no crazy markups, no stuffy showroom)
emails.preview: Your code's inside. Plus why 5,000+ people happily bought their sofa online.
keyPoints: Deliver the 15% first-order code prominently with a clear expiry and one primary button
Tell the brand story in the founder's voice: designer-quality looks without the luxury markups or gatekeeping showrooms
Disarm the 'I can't see or touch it' objection with 5,000+ five-star reviews, the 7-day return policy stated accurately, and color/scale guidance
Set delivery expectations: fast 1-4 business-day shipping from the LA warehouse, white-glove available locally

campaigns.name: Complete the Room (Post-Purchase Expansion)
campaigns.objective: Drive the second purchase by cross-selling complementary pieces while the first delivery's excitement is highest, growing lifetime value in a no-subscription repeat-purchase model.
campaigns.trigger: First order delivered (send ~7-14 days after delivery, once the piece is placed).
campaigns.segment: Recent first-time buyers; branch by the room category of the purchased item (e.g., bought a sofa, show living-room pairings).
campaigns.rationale: With no recurring revenue and high single-purchase drop-off, the room-completion cross-sell at peak satisfaction is the brand's single biggest expansion lever and replaces the playbook's renewal recap, which doesn't apply to one-off purchases.

emails.purpose: Celebrate the delivered piece and present a styled set of complementary items so the customer finishes the room with Statements by J rather than elsewhere.
emails.subject: Your new piece looks incredible — now let's finish the room
emails.preview: The pieces that pair perfectly with what you just bought — styled for you.
emails.cta: Shop pieces that complete the look
keyPoints: Acknowledge and celebrate the specific piece they received and the designer look they were going for
Show 3-4 complementary pieces matched to that room (rug, accent chair, lighting, console) as a curated 'finish the look' set
Lean on the styling-friend voice: how designers layer the room, with real shoppable products
Lower second-purchase risk by reminding them of the 7-day returns and that they already know the quality firsthand

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate past buyers and dormant subscribers who have gone quiet for 6-12 months and pull them back to furnish another room before they forget the brand.
campaigns.trigger: No purchase or email engagement for 6-12 months (or a long gap since first delivery).
campaigns.segment: Lapsed buyers (reference what they bought) vs. never-purchased dormant subscribers (lean on fresh value); sunset non-responders.
campaigns.rationale: High single-purchase drop-off and no existing re-engagement program means a large slice of the 5,000+ buyer base is sitting idle; win-back recovers 5-15% cheaply and protects list deliverability.

emails.purpose: Warmly re-open the relationship by showcasing new arrivals since their last visit plus a welcome-back incentive, giving a concrete reason to return.
emails.subject: It's been a while — here's what's new since your last visit
emails.preview: Fresh modern drops you haven't seen, plus a little something to welcome you back.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the gap warmly without guilt, in the brand's inclusive voice
Spotlight new monthly arrivals and a seasonal room-refresh hook so the brand feels current
Offer a returning-customer incentive (e.g., a welcome-back discount or open-box deal) as the reason to act now
Reinforce trust with the 5,000+ five-star reviews and easy 7-day returns to re-clear the no-touch objection

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter (New Arrivals & Styling)
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long, unpredictable repeat-purchase cycle by delivering design value and surfacing new monthly arrivals without leaning on discounts.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled monthly cadence; suppress contacts in active onboarding and route dormant ones to win-back.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; optionally branch editions by interest (small-space, designers, full-home movers).
campaigns.rationale: There is no blog or editorial cadence today, yet 'new arrivals monthly' is a natural recurring hook; an always-on value newsletter is the engine that keeps a one-off-purchase audience warm between buys.

emails.purpose: Flagship monthly edition: lead with one genuinely useful styling idea, then spotlight this month's new arrivals as the recurring habit-building section.
emails.subject: Just landed: this month's new arrivals (+ one styling trick)
emails.preview: Fresh modern pieces just dropped — plus how to make a small space feel designer.
keyPoints: Open with one standalone value idea (a styling/design tip, e.g., layering a small living room) — 80% value, 20% soft offer
Spotlight 3-5 new monthly arrivals with the price-accessible, anti-markup angle
Keep a single primary CTA and a recurring 'This month's drop' section that builds reader habit
Reinforce authority through real designer-style guidance, not promotions

campaigns.name: Showroom Booking & Reminders
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended showroom appointments for high-intent LA-area shoppers and designers, turning the free in-person visit into a faster close.
campaigns.trigger: Showroom appointment booked at the Commerce, CA location; reminder before the visit; recovery if no-show.
campaigns.segment: Local Los Angeles shoppers and interior designers who booked an appointment.
campaigns.rationale: The free showroom visit is this brand's only high-intent 'demo' and closes faster than online, but no automated confirmation/reminder exists; reminders cut no-shows ~35% and protect the highest-converting segment, though it serves only the local slice so it ranks below list-wide flows.

emails.purpose: Instant booking confirmation that locks in the visit, sets relaxed expectations, and primes the shopper to come prepared so the appointment converts.
emails.subject: You're booked — see you at the Commerce showroom
emails.preview: What to expect (zero pressure, zero stuffiness), plus parking and directions.
keyPoints: Confirm the date, time, and Commerce, CA address with an add-to-calendar link and a clear reschedule option
Set the anti-stuffy expectation: a relaxed, no-pressure visit to touch and feel the pieces in person
Tell them what to bring to make it productive (room dimensions, photos, inspiration, color swatches)
Note parking/directions and that white-glove local delivery is available if they buy
